terms.definition: Hyperbolic tangent activation tanh(x) = (e^x − e^(−x))/(e^x + e^(−x)), mapping inputs to (−1, 1) with tanh(0) = 0.
Full name for tanh; the S-shaped activation symmetric about the origin, used in RNN hidden states and LSTM candidate cells.
An activation with f(0) = 0 and odd symmetry f(−x) = −f(x); tanh is the canonical saturating example in neural networks.
Non-linearity whose output and gradient flatten for large |x|; tanh saturates toward ±1 and sigmoid toward 0 or 1.
When many tanh units saturate, 1 − tanh²(x) ≈ 0 and backpropagated gradients shrink, slowing learning in deep saturated stacks.
Function where f(−x) = −f(x); tanh is odd, which enforces zero-centered outputs for symmetric inputs.
